name: Lieferkettensorgfaltspflichtengesetz (German Supply Chain Due Diligence Act)
legislativeReference: Gesetz über die unternehmerischen Sorgfaltspflichten zur Vermeidung von Menschenrechtsverletzungen in Lieferketten

scope.description: German companies and companies with a registered branch in Germany

criteria.note: Companies with 3,000+ employees domiciled or with a branch office in Germany

scope.employeeCountNote: Includes temporary agency workers assigned for more than 6 months (Section 1(3))

dueDiligenceObligations.description: Risk-based due diligence obligations per Sections 3-10 LkSG

overarchingPrinciple.description: Obligation of effort, not of result (Bemühhenspflicht). Companies must take adequate measures based on the nature and extent of their business activity, their leverage, the severity of the expected adverse impact, the likelihood of occurrence, and the company's contribution to the adverse impact.

directSupplierObligations.description: Obligations with regard to direct suppliers (Sections 5-7)

riskAnalysis.name: Risk analysis (Risikoanalyse)
requirements: Ad-hoc risk analysis when the company must expect a significantly changed or expanded risk situation (e.g., new products, new business relationships, new country of operations)
Identify human rights and environmental risks in own business area and at direct suppliers
Weight and prioritise identified risks appropriately
Communicate findings of risk analysis to relevant decision-makers

ownBusinessArea.description: Measures within the company's own business area (Section 6(1)-(4))
measures: Policy statement on human rights strategy (Grundsatzerklärung)
Determine appropriate procurement practices to prevent or minimise identified risks
Implement preventive measures anchored in own business area (e.g., training, risk-based control measures)
Code of conduct: agree human rights and environmental expectations with suppliers
Procurement practices: include human rights clauses in supplier contracts
Training and capacity building for suppliers
Risk-based audits and inspections (contractual control mechanisms)
Agree upon contractual assurances from the direct supplier that it addresses human rights and environmental risks throughout its supply chain

remedialMeasures.name: Remedial measures (Abhilfemaßnahmen)
requirements: Take remedial action immediately once a violation of a human rights or environmental obligation at a direct supplier is identified or substantiated
Jointly develop and implement a plan to end or minimise the violation with the direct supplier
Temporary suspension of the business relationship during efforts to minimise risk
Termination of the business relationship as a last resort if the violation is very serious, attempts at remediation have failed after a set deadline, and no milder measures are available

indirectSupplierObligations.name: Indirect supplier obligations (mittelbare Zulieferer)
indirectSupplierObligations.trigger: Substantiated knowledge (substantiierte Kenntnis) of a possible violation at an indirect supplier
indirectSupplierObligations.description: Obligations are triggered only upon obtaining substantiated knowledge that a human rights or environmental violation at an indirect supplier may have occurred. This is a trigger-based, not ongoing, obligation.
requirements: Carry out a risk analysis without undue delay (Section 9(3) No. 1)
Establish appropriate preventive measures vis-à-vis the violating party (Section 9(3) No. 2)
Develop and implement a concept for preventing, ending, or minimising the violation (Section 9(3) No. 3)
Update the policy statement (Grundsatzerklärung) as necessary (Section 9(3) No. 4)

complaintsProcedure.name: Complaints procedure (Beschwerdeverfahren)
requirements: Establish an accessible complaints mechanism for persons to report human rights and environmental risks or violations
Procedure must be publicly accessible and clearly described
Independence of the person responsible for operating the procedure
Duty of confidentiality for the identity of the complainant
Effective protection against disadvantage or punishment for complainants
May be operated jointly with other companies or use external complaints mechanisms (e.g., industry-wide)

humanRightsRisks.description: Protected legal positions derived from international conventions listed in Section 2(2)

risks.name: Child labour
risks.description: Employment of children below minimum age (ILO C138) or in worst forms of child labour (ILO C182)

risks.name: Forced labour
risks.description: All forms of forced labour including slave labour, debt bondage, serfdom (ILO C29, C105)

risks.name: Discrimination
risks.description: Unequal treatment in employment based on national or ethnic origin, social origin, health status, disability, sexual orientation, age, sex, political opinion, religion, or beliefs (ILO C111)

risks.name: Freedom of association
risks.description: Failure to respect the right to form trade unions, the right to strike, and the right to collective bargaining (ILO C87, C98)

risks.name: Unsafe working conditions
risks.description: Failure to ensure occupational health and safety in accordance with the law of the place of employment, including exposure to hazardous substances, lack of protective equipment

risks.name: Fair wages
risks.description: Withholding an adequate wage — the wage defined by applicable law, which is at least the minimum wage defined by applicable law

risks.name: Harmful soil change, water or air pollution, harmful noise emission, excessive water consumption
risks.description: Environmental degradation causing impairment of the natural bases for the preservation and production of food, denial of access to safe drinking water, or harm to health

risks.name: Unlawful eviction or land taking
risks.description: Unlawful eviction or unlawful taking of land, forests, and waters whose use secures the livelihood of a person

risks.name: Use of private or public security forces
risks.description: Use of private or public security forces leading to violations due to lack of instruction or control

environmentalRisks.description: Environmental obligations derived from three international conventions

risks.name: Mercury and mercury waste (Minamata Convention)
risks.description: Production, use, handling of mercury and mercury compounds contrary to the Minamata Convention on Mercury

risks.name: Persistent organic pollutants (Stockholm Convention)
risks.description: Production, use, handling, collection, storage, and disposal of persistent organic pollutants contrary to the Stockholm Convention on POPs
references: Stockholm Convention on Persistent Organic Pollutants (2004)

risks.name: Hazardous waste export (Basel Convention)
risks.description: Import and export of hazardous wastes contrary to the Basel Convention
references: Basel Convention on the Control of Transboundary Movements of Hazardous Wastes (1992)

reporting.name: Annual reporting obligation (Berichtspflicht)
reporting.frequency: Annual, covering the preceding financial year
reporting.deadline: Within 4 months after end of financial year
reporting.publicDisclosure: Report must be published on the company's website free of charge for 7 years
contentRequirements: Whether and which human rights and environmental risks have been identified
Description of what the company has done to fulfil its due diligence obligations under Sections 4-9
Assessment of the impact and effectiveness of the measures taken

powers: Enter business premises and conduct inspections (Section 16)
Issue orders to take specific actions to fulfil obligations (Section 17)

fines.violation: Failure to comply with due diligence obligations (Sections 3-9)
fines.amount: Up to €8,000,000 or up to 2% of average annual worldwide turnover for companies with annual turnover exceeding €400 million

fines.violation: Failure to submit report or submitting materially incorrect report

publicProcurementExclusion.description: Exclusion from public procurement for up to 3 years if fined above certain thresholds (Section 22)
